page: Amazon Web Services c8in.48xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized instance powered by dedicated Intel processors. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It is highly optimized for network and Elastic Block Store performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.48xlarge offers high compute density and massive network throughput for large-scale deployments, making it a specialized choice where network performance and raw CPU power are prioritized over local storage capacity.

The hardware configuration of the c8in.48xlarge makes it suitable for compute-intensive workloads that require high network throughput. Appropriate use cases include distributed analytics, high-performance computing simulations, network appliances, communication workloads, and large-scale batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.48xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-heavy and EBS-optimized operations. This hardware profile is designed for high-compute density and massive network throughput. It is best suited for workloads such as high-performance computing, distributed analytics, network appliances, and intensive batch processing tasks that require substantial CPU resources and high-speed network connectivity without the need for local physical disks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 192 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput networking and intensive CPU workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8in.48xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized instance offering 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency compute instance featuring one hundred ninety-two virtual cores and three hundred gigabits per second of baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical Intel cores running at 3.9 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads
Nitro hypervisor architecture with dedicated CPU allocation
No local storage, optimized for network-attached storage
Designed for distributed analytics, HPC, and network appliances
